localTips.tip.generic.1.description: Some places may not accept cards.
localTips.tip.generic.1.title: Carry a small amount of cash
localTips.tip.gen.1.title: Carry some cash
localTips.tip.gen.1.description: Many local businesses only accept cash.
localTips.tip.gen.2.title: Share your itinerary
localTips.tip.gen.2.description: Share your trip plan with someone you trust before you leave.
localTips.tip.gen.3.title: Download offline maps
localTips.tip.gen.3.description: Download Google Maps or Maps.me to use without internet connection.
localTips.tip.gen.4.title: Agree on prices first
localTips.tip.gen.4.description: Always agree on the taxi or transport fare before you get in.
localTips.tip.gen.5.title: Respect local customs
localTips.tip.gen.5.description: Learn about local cultural norms before arriving to avoid misunderstandings.
localTips.tip.mx.1.title: Watch your belongings
localTips.tip.mx.1.description: Avoid showing valuables in busy tourist areas.
localTips.tip.mx.2.title: Exchange currency at banks
localTips.tip.mx.2.description: Banks and exchange offices offer better rates than the airport.
localTips.tip.mx.3.title: Use ride-sharing apps
localTips.tip.mx.3.description: InDrive and Uber are safer than hailing taxis off the street.
localTips.tip.mx.4.title: Don't drink tap water
localTips.tip.mx.4.description: Only drink bottled or purified water to avoid stomach issues.
localTips.tip.mx.5.title: Schedules are flexible
localTips.tip.mx.5.description: Restaurants and shops may open later than advertised. Be patient.
localTips.tip.mx.6.title: Connectivity
localTips.tip.mx.6.description: Telcel and AT&T have good coverage. Get a local SIM at the airport.
localTips.tip.mx.7.title: Sunscreen is essential
localTips.tip.mx.7.description: The Caribbean sun is intense. Use SPF50 and reapply every two hours.
localTips.tip.mx.8.title: Try local gastronomy
localTips.tip.mx.8.description: Tacos from local markets are more authentic and affordable than tourist spots.
localTips.tip.us.1.title: Tipping is expected
localTips.tip.us.1.description: Leave 18-20% at restaurants. It's part of the staff's income.
localTips.tip.us.2.title: A car is essential
localTips.tip.us.2.description: Public transport is limited in many cities. Consider renting a car.
localTips.tip.us.3.title: Keep copies of your documents
localTips.tip.us.3.description: Store digital copies of your passport and travel insurance in the cloud.
localTips.tip.us.4.title: Portions are huge
localTips.tip.us.4.description: Sharing dishes or asking for a doggy bag is totally normal.
localTips.tip.us.5.title: Type A & B plugs
localTips.tip.us.5.description: Outlets are 110V type A/B. If you're from Europe you'll need an adapter.
localTips.tip.us.6.title: Legal drinking age is 21
localTips.tip.us.6.description: Always carry ID if you plan to drink. It will be checked.
localTips.tip.es.1.title: Meal times are different
localTips.tip.es.1.description: Spaniards have lunch between 2pm and 4pm and dinner after 9pm.
localTips.tip.es.2.title: Siesta is real
localTips.tip.es.2.description: Many businesses close between 2pm and 5pm. Plan your visits accordingly.
localTips.tip.es.3.title: AVE trains are fast and punctual
localTips.tip.es.3.description: High-speed trains connect Madrid with major cities in just a few hours.
localTips.tip.es.4.title: Watch for pickpockets
localTips.tip.es.4.description: Barcelona and Madrid have high pickpocket rates in tourist areas.
localTips.tip.es.5.title: Euro is the currency
localTips.tip.es.5.description: Cards are accepted almost everywhere. Cash is useful at small bars and markets.
localTips.tip.it.1.title: Beware of pickpockets
localTips.tip.it.1.description: Keep your phone and wallet secure on public transport and in tourist areas.
localTips.tip.it.2.title: Validate your train ticket
localTips.tip.it.2.description: You must validate your ticket before boarding or risk a fine.
localTips.tip.it.3.title: Espresso at the bar
localTips.tip.it.3.description: Having coffee standing at the bar is cheaper and the Italian way.
localTips.tip.it.4.title: Dress code for churches
localTips.tip.it.4.description: Cover your shoulders and knees to enter churches and basilicas.
localTips.tip.it.5.title: Watch for cover charges
localTips.tip.it.5.description: Many restaurants charge a sitting fee (coperto). Check the menu.
localTips.tip.jp.1.title: Metro etiquette
localTips.tip.jp.1.description: Keep conversations low, don't eat, and follow platform queues.
localTips.tip.jp.2.title: Remove your shoes
localTips.tip.jp.2.description: You must remove shoes at many ryokans, temples and homes.
localTips.tip.jp.3.title: Japan is mostly cash-based
localTips.tip.jp.3.description: Carry yen in cash. Many small businesses don't accept cards.
localTips.tip.jp.4.title: Don't walk while eating
localTips.tip.jp.4.description: Eating while walking is considered rude in Japan.
localTips.tip.jp.5.title: Get a SIM or pocket WiFi
localTips.tip.jp.5.description: Public WiFi is limited. Rent a pocket WiFi device at the airport.
localTips.tip.fr.1.title: Say bonjour
localTips.tip.fr.1.description: It's polite to greet when entering a shop or restaurant with 'Bonjour'.
localTips.tip.fr.2.title: Fixed meal times
localTips.tip.fr.2.description: Restaurants serve lunch 12-2pm and dinner 7-10pm. Don't arrive outside these hours.
localTips.tip.fr.3.title: Paris metro is efficient
localTips.tip.fr.3.description: Buy a carnet of 10 trips or a Navigo card to save money.
localTips.tip.fr.4.title: Pickpockets in Paris
localTips.tip.fr.4.description: The Eiffel Tower and the metro are hotspots for pickpockets.
localTips.tip.fr.5.title: Euro is the currency
localTips.tip.fr.5.description: Cards are accepted almost everywhere. Keep some cash for markets.
localTips.tip.de.1.title: Germans value punctuality
localTips.tip.de.1.description: Arrive on time for reservations and appointments. It's strongly expected.
localTips.tip.de.2.title: Recycle strictly
localTips.tip.de.2.description: Recycling is mandatory. Separate paper, plastic, glass and organic waste.
localTips.tip.de.3.title: Cash is still king
localTips.tip.de.3.description: Many businesses only accept cash. Always carry euros.
localTips.tip.de.4.title: Bakeries open early
localTips.tip.de.4.description: A German bakery breakfast is a must-do cultural experience.
localTips.tip.de.5.title: Type F (Schuko) plugs
localTips.tip.de.5.description: Germany uses 230V type F plugs. Bring an adapter if you're from the Americas.
localTips.tip.gb.1.title: Drive on the left
localTips.tip.gb.1.description: Remember to look right before crossing the street. Traffic goes the other way.
localTips.tip.gb.2.title: Tipping is optional
localTips.tip.gb.2.description: 10-15% at restaurants is appreciated but not mandatory.
localTips.tip.gb.3.title: Always queue
localTips.tip.gb.3.description: British people take queuing very seriously. Never cut in line.
localTips.tip.gb.4.title: Fish and chips is essential
localTips.tip.gb.4.description: Try fish and chips from a traditional chip shop, not a tourist restaurant.
localTips.tip.gb.5.title: Type G plugs
localTips.tip.gb.5.description: The UK uses three-pin type G plugs. You'll need an adapter.
localTips.tip.th.1.title: Respect the monarchy
localTips.tip.th.1.description: Speaking negatively about the royal family is illegal in Thailand. Avoid it entirely.
localTips.tip.th.2.title: Watch out for tourist scams
localTips.tip.th.2.description: Be wary of strangers offering cheap tours or claiming attractions are closed.
localTips.tip.th.3.title: Bargain at markets
localTips.tip.th.3.description: At markets and shops without fixed prices, bargaining is expected and normal.
localTips.tip.th.4.title: Street food is excellent
localTips.tip.th.4.description: Busy stalls full of locals are a sign of good quality food.
localTips.tip.th.5.title: Fix tuk-tuk prices beforehand
localTips.tip.th.5.description: Always agree on the price before getting into a tuk-tuk to avoid surprises.
localTips.tip.ar.1.title: Currency exchange
localTips.tip.ar.1.description: Exchange USD at official exchange offices or use the tourist dollar rate.
localTips.tip.ar.2.title: Dinner is served late
localTips.tip.ar.2.description: Argentinians eat dinner after 9pm. Restaurants open late.
localTips.tip.ar.3.title: Mate is culture
localTips.tip.ar.3.description: If someone offers you mate, accepting it is a sign of hospitality.
localTips.tip.ar.4.title: Buenos Aires with caution
localTips.tip.ar.4.description: Avoid showing jewelry or phones on the street and be alert.
localTips.tip.ar.5.title: SUBE card for transport
localTips.tip.ar.5.description: You need the SUBE card to use public transport in Buenos Aires.
localTips.tip.co.1.title: Avoid unofficial taxis
localTips.tip.co.1.description: Use InDrive or Cabify apps instead of flagging down taxis on the street.
localTips.tip.co.2.title: Ajiaco and bandeja paisa
localTips.tip.co.2.description: These are iconic dishes you must try. Each region has its own specialty.
localTips.tip.co.3.title: Colombian peso
localTips.tip.co.3.description: Exchange money at official banks or ATMs. Avoid street money changers.
localTips.tip.co.4.title: Very friendly locals
localTips.tip.co.4.description: Colombians are very welcoming. Don't hesitate to ask for help.
localTips.tip.co.5.title: Bogota altitude
localTips.tip.co.5.description: Bogota is at 2600m. Rest your first day, drink water and avoid alcohol.
localTips.tip.br.1.title: Protect your belongings at the beach
localTips.tip.br.1.description: Don't leave valuables unattended on the beach. Take only what you need.
localTips.tip.br.2.title: Brazilian real
localTips.tip.br.2.description: Withdraw money from ATMs at recognized banks to avoid skimmers.
localTips.tip.br.3.title: Acai and caipirinhas
localTips.tip.br.3.description: Acai with granola and caipirinha are culinary experiences you can't miss.
localTips.tip.br.4.title: Portuguese is different
localTips.tip.br.4.description: Spanish won't get you far here. Learn some basic Portuguese phrases.
localTips.tip.br.5.title: Sao Paulo traffic is intense
localTips.tip.br.5.description: Plan your commutes in advance. The metro is the best option within the city.
localTips.tip.au.1.title: Extreme sun
localTips.tip.au.1.description: Australian sun is very intense. Wear SPF50, a hat and avoid peak hours.
localTips.tip.au.2.title: Wildlife can be dangerous
localTips.tip.au.2.description: Respect wildlife warning signs. Jellyfish, snakes and spiders can be hazardous.
localTips.tip.au.3.title: Australian dollar
localTips.tip.au.3.description: Cards are accepted almost everywhere. ATMs usually charge a fee.
localTips.tip.au.4.title: Brunch is a ritual
localTips.tip.au.4.description: Australians love coffee and brunch. Find a local café for the full experience.
localTips.tip.au.5.title: Type I plugs
localTips.tip.au.5.description: Australia uses 230V type I plugs. You'll need an adapter.
localTips.tip.pe.1.title: Altitude sickness in Cusco
localTips.tip.pe.1.description: Cusco is at 3400m. Rest your first day, drink coca tea and avoid alcohol.
localTips.tip.pe.2.title: Machu Picchu requires advance booking
localTips.tip.pe.2.description: Tickets sell out months in advance. Book early.
localTips.tip.pe.3.title: Ceviche is the national dish
localTips.tip.pe.3.description: Try ceviche at a local restaurant at lunchtime when it's freshest.
localTips.tip.pe.4.title: Strong Peruvian sun
localTips.tip.pe.4.description: The sun in the highlands and coast is intense. Always use sunscreen.
localTips.tip.pe.5.title: Negotiate taxi prices
localTips.tip.pe.5.description: Taxis without meters require price negotiation before you get in.
localTips.noTips: No tips available for this destination yet.
localTips.tip.rome.1.description: Keep your phone and wallet secure in public transport.
localTips.tip.rome.1.title: Pickpockets in crowded areas
localTips.tip.tokyo.1.description: Keep conversations low and follow platform lines.
localTips.tip.tokyo.1.title: Metro etiquette
localTips.title: Local tips
